sec.method: calculated: 0.708 g/L (8 mM) butyrate formed after ~356 h of 1-L bioreactor batch fermentation under CO2-H2 conditions; rate = 8 mmol/L / 356 h = 0.0225 mmol/L/h (volumetric, not biomass-normalized; no gDW/L given in paper)

growth_records.cite: Shin et al. Genome-wide CRISPRi screen identifies enhanced autolithotrophic phenotypes in acetogenic bacterium Eubacterium limosum. Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences of the United States of America (2023). DOI:10.1073/pnas.2216244120. [PMC9963998]
growth_records.snippet: When acetate reached approximately 2.2 g/L (36 mM), butyrate formation was observed. Finally, we observed that 0.708 g/L (8 mM) butyrate was formed after approximately 356 h of fermentation under CO2-H2 growth conditions
growth_records.notes: Volumetric productivity only; biomass (gDCW/L) not reported for this bioreactor run so a specific (per-gDW) secretion rate could not be computed. Rate = total butyrate titer / total elapsed fermentation time (crude average, ignores lag/growth phase). Growth conditions: 1-L bioreactor, CO2:H2 = 20:80, 200 kPa (batch fermentation confirming BCD-EtfAB butyrogenesis).

growth_records.medium: autotrophic H2/CO2 medium (composition in SI Appendix)

up.method: measured consumption rate during autotrophic growth (Fig 4F)
measured consumption rate during autotrophic growth (Fig 4F)

sec.method: measured production rate during autotrophic growth (Fig 4G)

growth_records.cite: Song Y et al. Functional cooperation of the glycine synthase-reductase and Wood-Ljungdahl pathways for autotrophic growth of Clostridium drakei. Proc Natl Acad Sci U S A (2020). DOI:10.1073/pnas.1912289117. [PMC7132306]
growth_records.snippet: the growth rate of the GSRP strain was faster than that of the control strain, with growth rates of 0.00719 and 0.00523 h-1, respectively ... the consumption rates of H2 and CO2 by the GSRP strain were 2.291 and 0.276 mmol.gDW-1.h-1 ... 0.9
growth_records.notes: E. limosum ATCC 8486 CONTROL strain (empty plasmid backbone), autotrophic H2/CO2. All rates MEASURED/experimental. growth 0.00523 h-1; doubling = ln2/0.00523 = 132.5 h (calculated). H2 uptake 0.943, CO2 uptake 0.191, acetate secretion 0.559 mmol/gDW/h. Temperature/pH/medium composition not in main text (SI Appendix). Engineered-host control strain but rates are genuine experimental measurements.

growth_records.strain: ATCC 8486 + C. drakei GSRP gene cluster (GSRP strain)
growth_records.medium: autotrophic H2/CO2 medium (composition in SI Appendix)

up.method: measured consumption rate during autotrophic growth (Fig 4F)
measured consumption rate during autotrophic growth (Fig 4F)

sec.method: measured production rate during autotrophic growth (Fig 4G)

growth_records.cite: Song Y et al. Functional cooperation of the glycine synthase-reductase and Wood-Ljungdahl pathways for autotrophic growth of Clostridium drakei. Proc Natl Acad Sci U S A (2020). DOI:10.1073/pnas.1912289117. [PMC7132306]
growth_records.snippet: growth rates of 0.00719 and 0.00523 h-1 ... the consumption rates of H2 and CO2 by the GSRP strain were 2.291 and 0.276 mmol.gDW-1.h-1 ... the acetate production rate by the GSRP strain ... with 1.191 and 0.559 mmol.gDW-1.h-1
growth_records.notes: E. limosum ATCC 8486 GSRP strain (expresses C. drakei GSRP genes), autotrophic H2/CO2. All rates MEASURED/experimental. growth 0.00719 h-1; doubling = ln2/0.00719 = 96.4 h (calculated). H2 uptake 2.291, CO2 uptake 0.276, acetate secretion 1.191 mmol/gDW/h. Higher C1 consumption and growth than control demonstrates functional GSRP. Temperature/pH/medium composition not in main text (SI Appendix).

phenotypes.base_medium: Sole-substrate growth / assimilation on a defined minimal medium (substrate as C/N/energy source) — positive = the organism grows on it
